trait.name: Dive Attack
entries: If the aarakocra is flying and dives at least 30 feet straight toward a target and then hits it with a melee weapon attack, the attack deals an extra 3 (1d6) damage to the target.

action.name: Talon
entries: {@atk mw} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: {@damage 4(1d4+2)} slashing damage.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (2d6 + 3) slashing damage plus 3 (1d6) acid damage. {@condition grappled} If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it is grappled (escape DC 13). Until this grapple ends, the ankheg can bite only the grappled creature and has advantage on attack rolls to do so.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The death knight has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The dragon makes two attacks with its bite or tail, or one attack with its bite and one with its claw.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The dragon can breathe air and water.

action.name: Bite
entries: {@atk mw} +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d10 + 3) piercing damage.

trait.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: The drow has advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put the drow to sleep.

trait.name: Invisible Tricksters
entries: The only warning of a faerie dragon's presence is a stifled giggle. The dragon's invisible until its fun is done, depending on the disposition of its prey.

trait.name: False Appearance
entries: While the violet fungus remains motionless, it is indistinguishable from an ordinary fungus.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The fungus makes 1d4 Rotting Touch attacks.

action.name: Rotting Touch
entries: {@atk mw} +2 to hit, reach 10 ft., one creature. Hit: {@damage 1d8} necrotic damage.

trait.name: Elemental Demise
entries: If the dao dies, its body disintegrates into crystalline powder, leaving behind only equipment the dao was wearing or carrying.

items: At will: mage hand (the hand is invisible)
3/day each: jump, misty step, nondetection (self only)

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The githyanki makes two greatsword attacks.

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting (Psionics)
entries: The githyanki's innate spellcasting ability is Intelligence (spell save DC 13, +5 to hit with spell attacks). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components: At will: mage hand (the hand is invisible); 3/day each: jump, misty step, nondetection (self only), tongues; 1/day each: plane shift, telekinesis.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The githyanki makes two silver greatsword attacks.

ac.condition: wearing no armor and wielding no shield

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting (Psionics)
entries: Spell save DC 14, +6 to hit with spell attacks. The githzerai can innately cast the following spells, requiring no components: At will: mage hand (the hand is invisible). 3/day each: feather fall, jump, see invisibility, shield. 1/day each: phantasmal killer, plane shift.

trait.name: Rampage
entries: When the gnoll reduces a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack on its turn, the gnoll can take a bonus action to move up to half its speed and make a bite attack.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gnoll makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws.

trait.name: Rampage
entries: When the gnoll reduces a creature to 0 hit points with a melee attack on its turn, the gnoll can take a bonus action to move up to half its speed and make a bite attack.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The gnoll makes two attacks, either with its glaive or its longbow, and uses its Incite Rampage if it can.

trait.name: Stone Camouflage
entries: The gnome has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide in rocky terrain.

languages: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Berserk
entries: Whenever the golem starts its turn with 40 hit points or fewer, roll a d6. On a 6, the golem goes berserk. On each of its turns while berserk, the golem attacks the nearest creature it can see. If no creature is near enough to move to and attack, the golem attacks an object, with preference for an object smaller than itself. Once the golem goes berserk, it continues to do so until it is destroyed or regains all its hit points. The golem's creator, if within 60 feet of the berserk golem, can try to calm it by speaking firmly and persuasively. The golem must be able to hear its creator, who must take an action to make a DC 15 Charisma (Persuasion) check. If the check succeeds, the golem ceases being berserk. If it takes damage while still at 40 hit points or fewer, the golem might go berserk again.

action.name: Trampling Charge
entries: When you make a melee attack on your turn, you can move up to your speed toward the target and then attack it again as a bonus action. If you're wielding a weapon, you can make two attacks with it on your turn, one with your action and one with your bonus action.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: Can breathe air and water.

trait.name: Amphibious
entries: The hag can breathe air and water.

languageTags: understands the languages of its creator but can't speak

trait.name: Martial Advantage
entries: Once per turn, the hobgoblin can deal an extra 14 (4d6) damage to a creature it hits with a weapon attack if that creature is within 5 feet of an ally of the hobgoblin that isn't incapacitated.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The hobgoblin makes three melee attacks. Alternatively, it can make two ranged attacks with its javelins.

trait.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: While in sunlight, the kobold has disadvantage on attack rolls, as well as on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

trait.name: Amphibious

trait.name: Hold Breath
entries: The lizardfolk can hold its breath for 15 minutes.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The lizardfolk makes two melee attacks, each one with a different weapon.

trait.name: Tail Spike Regrowth
entries: The manticore has twenty-four tail spikes. Used spikes regrow when the manticore finishes a long rest.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The manticore makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws or three with its tail spikes.

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The mind flayer has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Distress Spores
entries: When the myconid takes damage, all other myconids within 240 feet of it can sense its pain.

action.name: Fist

trait.name: Innate Spellcasting
entries: The oni's innate spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 13). The oni can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components: {@list} At will: darkness, invisibility; 1/day each: charm person, cone of cold, gaseous form, sleep.

action.name: Keen Sight
entries: The roc has advantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks that rely on sight.

action.name: Magic Resistance

trait.name: Magic Resistance
entries: The slaad has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

trait.name: Shapechanger
entries: The slaad can use its action to polymorph into a Small or Medium humanoid, or back into its true form. Its statistics, other than its size, are the same in each form. Any equipment it is wearing or carrying isn't transformed. It reverts to its true form if it dies.

trait.name: Chameleon Carapace
entries: Can change color to match surroundings. Advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

trait.name: Confusing Gaze
entries: When a creature starts its turn within 30 feet of the umber hulk and is able to see the umber hulk's eyes, the umber hulk can magically force it to make a DC 15 Charisma saving throw, unless the umber hulk is incapacitated. On a failed saving throw, the creature can't take reactions until the start of its next turn and rolls a d8 to determine what it does during that turn. On a 1-4, the creature does nothing. On a 5-6, the creature takes no action but uses all its movement to move in a random direction. On a 7-8, the creature makes one melee attack against a random creature, or it does nothing if no creature is within reach. Unless surprised, a creature can avert its eyes to avoid the saving throw at the start of its turn. If the creature does so, it can't see the umber hulk until the start of its next turn, when it can avert its eyes again. If the creature looks at the umber hulk in the meantime, it must immediately make the save.

entities.name: Animated Objects
entities.canonicalText: This text describes general rules and mechanics for animated objects, not a specific discrete game entity like a monster or item.

entities.name: Flying Sword
entities.canonicalText: The text describes animated weapons in general, not a specific discrete game entity with its own stat block or rules.

legendary.name: Legendary Actions
entries: Detect. The dracolich makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.
Tail Attack. The dracolich makes a tail attack.

Wing Attack (Costs 2 Actions). The dracolich beats its tattered wings. Each creature within 10 feet of the dracolich must succeed on a DC 21 Dexterity saving throw or take 14 (2d6 + 7) bludgeoning damage and be knocked prone. After beating its wings this way, the dracolich can fly up to half its flying speed.

entities.name: Dragon, Shadow
entities.canonicalText: Narrative text describing shadow dragons' lore and transformation, not a discrete game entity with stat-block data.

entities.name: Shadow Dragon Template
entities.canonicalText: The text describes a template for creating shadow dragons but is not a discrete game entity. It contains rules and mechanics rather than a standalone entity.

entities.name: Dragon Agecate Gories
entities.canonicalText: The text is a table describing dragon age categories and size ranges, not a discrete game entity. It appears as part of the Monster Manual's rules for categorizing dragons by age and size.

entities.name: Varian T: Drag Ons as Innate Spellcasters
entities.canonicalText: Describes a variant rule for dragons (game mechanic) rather than a discrete entity, outlining innate spellcasting abilities as a variant option.

entities.name: \• Chromait'ic Dragons
entities.canonicalText: The text provides lore and narrative about chromatic dragons and Tiamat, but no discrete game entity (stat-block, monster, or item) is described.

entities.name: Black Dragon

entries: Crackles with static when angry or attacking.

entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +14 to hit, reach 60 ft., one target. Hit: 50 (8d10 + 20) lightning damage. The target must succeed on a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, or be stunned until the end of its next turn.

legendary.name: Desert Camouflage
entries: Can blend into desert sands, appearing as stone.

legendary.name: Hoarder's Insight
entries: Can sense nearby valuable items and creatures.

spellcasting.name: Arcane Spells
entries: Caster level 14th. Spellcasting ability Wisdom (spell save DC 17). The dragon can cast the following spells: 3/day of 3rd level or lower, 2/day of 4th level or lower, 1/day of 5th level or lower.

entities.name: A GREEN DRAGON'S LAIR

trait.name: Arrogant Tyrants
entries: Fly into destructive rages and act on impulse when angered.
Regarded as the archetypal evil dragon by many cultures.

trait.name: Obsessive Collectors
entries: Value wealth above all else; treasure hoards are legendary.
Can judge the worth of a bauble to within a copper piece at a glance.
Knows the value and provenance of every item in its hoard.

trait.name: Status and Slaves
entries: Fiercely territorial and isolationist, but uses lesser creatures as informants/messengers.
Demands fealty from chaotic evil humanoids; slaughters leaders to claim lordship.
Servants live in terror of being roasted and eaten; spend time fawning to stay alive.
Odor of sulfur and pumice surrounds it; swept-back horns and spinal frill define its silhouette.
Beaked snout vents smoke constantly; eyes dance with flame when angry.
Wings are longest of chromatic dragons with blue-black trailing edge resembling metal.

action.name: Breath Weapon
entries: 150-foot cone of fire; 60 damage (10d6 fire), DC 21 save for half damage.
Can use this ability 3 times, then must recharge.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: Make two attacks: one with its bite and one with its tail, or two tail attacks.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target.
Damage: 20 (3d10 + 5) piercing damage, and 15 (3d8 + 3) fire damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +11 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target.
Damage: 15 (2d10 + 5) bludgeoning damage, and 10 (2d6 + 3) fire damage.

legendary.name: Molten Fury
entries: When reduced to 0 HP, it can use its breath weapon again as a reaction.

legendary.name: Treasure Sense
entries: Knows the value and location of every item in its hoard; notices absence of a single coin.

spellcasting.name: Divine Magic
entries: Spellcasting Ability: Wisdom (spell save DC 17).
Spells: Cancast 6th-level spells from the Draconic spell list.

variant.name: Drider Spellcasting
variant.description: Driders that were once drow spellcasters might retain their ability to cast spells. Such driders typically have a higher spellcasting ability (15 or 16) than other driders. Further, the drider gains the Spellcasting trait.

entities.name: Fire Elemental
entities.canonicalText: The provided text is a narrative description of a fire elemental's behavior and effects, not a structured game entity entry.

entities.name: Drow Elite Warrior
entities.canonicalText: Descriptive passage about drow elite warriors' role and behavior, lacks mechanical game data

trait.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: The drow has advantage on saving throws against being charmed, and magic can't put the drow to sleep.

action.name: Innate Spellcasting
entries: The drow's spellcasting ability is Charisma (spell save DC 11). It can innately cast the following spells, requiring no material components: At will: dancing lights. 1/day each: darkness, faerie fire.

entities.name: The Ordning
entities.canonicalText: The text describes a cultural/social system (the ordning) among giants, not a discrete game entity like a monster, item, or spell. It contains narrative and lore rather than actionable rules or statistics.

trait.name: Fire Forged
entries: Fortresses built around volcanoes; walls glow orange; use coal or wood in cold regions

trait.name: Martial Experts
entries: Disciplined combat training; songs and dances of battle; shrewd tacticians

trait.name: Skilled Artisans
entries: Master smiths; create potent siege weapons; experiment with alloys

action.name: Magma Jet
entries: Ranged 60 ft., 2d6 fire damage; can target one creature or 10 ft. cone

action.name: Boulder Throw
entries: Ranged 120 ft., 4d6 bludgeoning damage; can target one creature or 10 ft. cone

legendary.name: Feudal Lords
entries: Conquer and enslave humanoid serfs; manage mines and slaves; prioritize smelting over safety

It modifies existing creatures by adding features like blindsight, damage resistance, and breath weapons, but does not represent a standalone entity.

entities.name: Variant: Thri-Kreen Weapons and Psionics
entities.canonicalText: The text describes weapon and psionic abilities of the Thri-Kreen monster, which are part of its stat block rather than standalone entities. It contains no discrete game entity outside of being variant details for the Thri-Kreen monster.

trait.name: Chameleon Carapace
entries: The thri-kreen can change the color of its carapace to match the color and texture of its surroundings. As a result, it has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks made to hide.

entities.name: AcTIONS
entities.canonicalText: The text includes both action descriptions (Slam, Sting, Club) and narrative explanations about awakened trees and acolytes, which are not a single discrete game entity.

entities.name: Other Animals
entities.canonicalText: This is a section heading explaining how to use existing stat blocks for animals, not a discrete game entity.

entities.name: Variant: Giant Lizard Traits
entities.canonicalText: This text describes variant traits for giant lizards rather than a discrete game entity.

trait.name: Pack Tactics
entries: The shark has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the shark's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

trait.name: Water Breathing
entries: The shark can breathe only underwater.

entities.name: The Nature of Swarms

entities.name: CusTOMIZING NPCs

entities.name: Variant: Familiars
entities.canonicalText: The text explains game rules for familiars (a player option involving creatures from the Player's Handbook or other Tiny monsters) rather than describing a specific monster entry from the Monster Manual.
